title: Cesium Chloride Gradients for Molecular Separation
author: Matthew Sullivan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.c7dzi5

steps.title: Prepare CsCl densities
steps.instruction: Prepare cesium chloride densities of p1.2, p1.4, p1.5 and p1.65 in seawater (sw) that has been 0.02μm (or 0.2μm) filtered and autoclaved.

steps.title: Place tube on balance
steps.instruction: Place tube of prepared cesium on balance

steps.title: Tare balance
steps.instruction: Tare balance to 0

steps.title: Verify CsCl density
steps.instruction: Remove 1ml with sterile pipet tip. p1.2 11.19g per 50ml seawater p1.4 26.94g per 50ml seawater p1.5 33.74g per 50ml seawater p1.65 43.78g per 50ml seawater Difference in weight should be equal to the density of the solution (eg, 1ml of p1.2 should weigh 1.2ml). Adjust as needed.

substeps.instruction: Remove 1ml with sterile pipet tip. p
43.78g per 50ml seawater Difference in weight should be equal to the density of the solution (eg, 1ml of p

steps.title: Layer CsCl densities
steps.instruction: Layer CsCl from the bottom using a gradient fractionator if you have one or by hand: SW40 rotor: 2ml p1.65 3ml p1.5 3ml p1.4 1ml p1.2 SW28 rotor: 5ml p1.65 8ml p1.5 9ml p1.4 3ml p1.65 The p1.2 layer is to equilibrate sample before it hits the p1.4 cesium layer. Use thin-walled SW40 or SW28 tubes! This was done with a fraction collector (Labconco) but you can carefully layer these solutions with Pasteur pipets.

layer is to equilibrate sample before it hits the p
cesium layer. Use thin-walled SW40 or SW28 tubes! This was done with a fraction collector (Labconco) but you can carefully layer these solutions with Pasteur pipets.

steps.title: Layer sample
steps.instruction: Carefully layer on sample (~5ml can be layered with SW40, ~15ml with SW28). Use thin-walled SW40 or SW28 tubes! This was done with a fraction collector (Labconco) but you can carefully layer these solutions with Pasteur pipets.

steps.title: Weigh and balance tubes
steps.instruction: Weigh and balance tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge using SW40 or SW28 Beckman rotor @ 24,000rpm for 4hr at 4°C. With a SW-48 swinging-bucket rotor (Beckman), setting 9 was used for deceleration. This slower deceleration should reduce mixing in your gradient.

steps.title: Label collection tubes
steps.instruction: Have sterile 0.5ml tubes (SW40) or 1.5ml tubes (SW28) labeled 1-24

steps.title: Place tubes in rack
steps.instruction: Open tubes in a rack.

steps.title: Place gradient tube on stand
steps.instruction: Place tube on ring stand

steps.title: Puncture gradient tube
steps.instruction: Puncture about 2mm from the bottom side of the tube using a sterile 20 guage needle, bevel up. To make things easier, can pull off top sample layer with pipette as it will not contain any viruses after centrifugation; but drops will slow down as you reach the end of the collection.

steps.title: Collect fractions
steps.instruction: Collect droplets to fill each tube in order. Do not need to collect bottom layer as virus will not be in that layer.

steps.title: Prepare for density measurement
steps.instruction: Place tube with sample on 4-place balance in holder. Make one by cutting off top 1" of 15cc tube with cap on.

steps.title: Tare balance
steps.instruction: Tare to 0.

steps.title: Remove 100µl sample
steps.instruction: With sterile pipet tip, remove 100µl.

steps.title: Take measurement
steps.instruction: Take measurement.

steps.title: Return sample
steps.instruction: Return sample to tube.

steps.title: Repeat 100µl measurement (1st repeat)
steps.instruction: Remove another 100µl, take measurement, return sample to tube.

steps.title: Repeat 100µl measurement (2nd repeat)
steps.instruction: Remove yet another 100µl, take measurement, and return sample to tube.
